user_message: Just got this from Eric Kim at Coho Winery -- I need help figuring out whitespace analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 14:13 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 14:30 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 16:41 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Coho Winery: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-A trap (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-A failure mode (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-A -- Loses a commitment buried in a side thread
